conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, let me review the official eligibility rules so I can provide you with accurate guidance.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now I can help assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, do you have any children that you are responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I have one child. My son Alex is 8 years old and he lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. It's helpful to know that you have Alex, who is 8 years old and lives with you.

To continue assessing your eligibility, do you currently live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK at the moment. I'm currently living in France.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since you're living in France, there are specific rules that apply.

Could you tell me if any of the following circumstances apply to you:

1. Are you a Crown servant (for example, working for the UK government abroad)?
2. Do you have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme?
3. Are you an EEA or Swiss national, or a family member of one?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of those apply to me. I'm not a Crown servant, I don't have any status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and I'm not an EEA or Swiss national or a family member of one. I just moved abroad for work a little while ago.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. I have enough details now to make a determination about your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on the information you've provided and the official UK government rules, let me explain the outcome:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Unfortunately, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why: While Alex meets the age requirement (he's under 16) and lives with you, one of the key eligibility requirements for Child Benefit is that you must live in the UK. Since you're currently living in France, you don't meet this fundamental requirement.

There are some exceptions that allow people living abroad to claim Child Benefit, such as:
- Being a Crown servant (working for the UK government abroad)
- Having settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme with certain additional conditions
- Being in certain other specific circumstances

However, based on what you've told me, none of these exceptions apply to your situation.

If your circumstances change in the future - for example, if you move back to the UK - you would be able to claim Child Benefit at that time.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old and lives with you, which would normally meet the age requirement (under 16) and responsibility requirement for Child Benefit. However, the eligibility rules state that you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 AND you live in the UK. You are currently living in France and do not meet any of the exceptions that would allow you to claim Child Benefit while living abroad. The exceptions include being a Crown servant or having certain immigration status under the EU Settlement Scheme, none of which apply to your situation. Therefore, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex while living in France.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (aged 8), who lives with them. While the child meets the age requirement and lives with the claimant, the claimant is currently living in France and does not meet any of the exceptions that would allow claiming Child Benefit from abroad (not a Crown servant, no settled/pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme, not an EEA/Swiss national or family member). The fundamental requirement to live in the UK is not met, and no exceptions apply.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 8 years old and lives with you, which would normally meet the age requirement (under 16) and responsibility requirement for Child Benefit. However, the eligibility rules state that you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 AND you live in the UK. You are currently living in France and do not meet any of the exceptions that would allow you to claim Child Benefit while living abroad. The exceptions include being a Crown servant or having certain immigration status under the EU Settlement Scheme, none of which apply to your situation. Therefore, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex while living in France.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (aged 8), who lives with them. While the child meets the age requirement and lives with the claimant, the claimant is currently living in France and does not meet any of the exceptions that would allow claiming Child Benefit from abroad (not a Crown servant, no settled/pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme, not an EEA/Swiss national or family member). The fundamental requirement to live in the UK is not met, and no exceptions apply.
